title: Pakistani Daal Chawal

subs.name: 250 g chana daal or toor daal
subs.notes: chana daal needs an extra 15 minutes of cooking; toor daal is very similar

subs.name: Sona Masoori rice
subs.notes: reduce water slightly as it absorbs less

subs.name: 2 tsp butter or extra oil
subs.notes: ghee tarka gives the most authentic smoky aroma

steps.text: Wash the daal in several changes of water until the water runs clear. Wash and soak the basmati rice in cold water for 20 minutes, then cook with 1.5 times its volume of water and a pinch of salt in a covered pot on low heat for 15 minutes until perfectly fluffy. Rest covered, off heat.
Heat 2 tablespoons of oil in a medium pot. Add the chopped onion and fry on medium heat for 6–7 minutes until golden. Add ginger-garlic paste and cook for 1 minute until the raw smell disappears.

Add tomatoes, turmeric, coriander powder, and red chilli powder. Stir and cook for 3 minutes until the tomatoes soften. Add the washed daal, green chillies, and 700 ml water. Bring to a boil, then simmer on low heat for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the lentils are fully dissolved and creamy.

Adjust salt to taste. If the daal is thicker than you like, stir in a little hot water. The consistency should be thick but pourable — it should flow off the spoon in a slow, steady stream rather than clumping.

For the tarka (tempering), heat ghee and 1 tablespoon oil in a small pan over high heat until smoking. Add the cumin seeds and dried red chillies. Within 10 seconds they will splutter and darken — immediately pour this sizzling tarka over the daal and cover for 30 seconds to let the aroma bloom.
Garnish with fresh coriander and a squeeze of lemon juice. Serve the daal in a bowl alongside a generous serving of steamed basmati rice, with a pickle and raw onion on the side.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: masoor-dal → 250 g chana daal or toor daal (chana daal needs an extra 15 minutes of cooking; toor daal is very similar); ghee → 2 tsp butter or extra oil (ghee tarka gives the most authentic smoky aroma); basmati-rice → Sona Masoori rice (reduce water slightly as it absorbs less)
indian_kitchen_adaptation: Pakistani daal chawal is virtually identical to the north Indian version. Masoor daal is widely available at all Indian provision stores. For a pressure cooker version, cook the tempered masala with washed daal and 500 ml water for 3 whistles on medium heat — open naturally and adjust consistency. The ghee tarka is an Indian technique too — this dish needs no adaptation at all.
